summary: PH: Recruiter sees error messages if their email has bounced
wiki:
h2. User story

*As a* recruiter
*I want* clear bounce diagnostics on affected outbound
*So that* I correct addressability issues quickly (transcript: bounce in list discussion)

h2. Scenarios

All scenarios below assume the recruiter is viewing the *reading pane* for an outbound that was not delivered. The headline is the same for every variant; the body copy matches the failure classification.

h3. Scenario 1: Reading pane — generic / default not-delivered

*Given* the failure is shown with the generic not-delivered classification
*When* I view the message in the reading pane
*Then* I see the headline: We couldn't deliver this email.
*And* I see the body: Try sending it again or contact the recipient using another method.

h3. Scenario 2: Reading pane — spam variant

*Given* the failure is classified as spam / sender-reputation (recipient marked a previous email as spam)
*When* I view the message in the reading pane
*Then* I see the headline: We couldn't deliver this email.
*And* I see the body: The recipient marked a previous email as spam. Avoid emailing this address to protect your sender reputation, and contact the recipient using another method.

h3. Scenario 3: Reading pane — unsubscribe variant

*Given* the failure is classified as unsubscribe / consent
*When* I view the message in the reading pane
*Then* I see the headline: We couldn't deliver this email.
*And* I see the body: The recipient unsubscribed from your emails. You'll need their explicit opt-in before you can email them again. Try contacting them using another method.

h3. Scenario 4: Reading pane — virus variant

*Given* the failure is classified as virus / unsafe content
*When* I view the message in the reading pane
*Then* I see the headline: We couldn't deliver this email.
*And* I see the body: This email was blocked because it contained content that was flagged as a virus. Remove any attachments and try sending it again.

h3. Scenario 5: Reading pane — template variant

*Given* the failure is classified as template or formatting issue
*When* I view the message in the reading pane
*Then* I see the headline: We couldn't deliver this email.
*And* I see the body: We couldn't deliver your email because of a template or formatting issue. Try sending it again without a template, or contact the recipient using another method.

h3. Scenario 6: Reading pane — server variant

*Given* the failure is classified as recipient server temporarily unavailable (retry in progress)
*When* I view the message in the reading pane
*Then* I see the headline: We couldn't deliver this email.
*And* I see the body: The recipient's server is temporarily unavailable. We'll automatically keep trying, but if it's urgent, contact them using another method.

h3. Scenario 7: Reading pane — technical issue variant

*Given* the failure is classified as a technical issue without a more specific variant
*When* I view the message in the reading pane
*Then* I see the headline: We couldn't deliver this email.
*And* I see the body: We couldn't deliver your email because of a technical issue. If the issue persists, use another method to reach the recipient or contact support.

h3. Scenario 8: Bounce detail stays private across recruiters

*Given* I lack access to the underlying candidate or thread
*When* another user's outbound fails
*Then* I do not see that recruiter's private bounce copy in my reading pane

h2. Definition of Ready

||Criterion||Any blocking issues to being ready / Notes||
|We understand the value this work provides to customers and how it supports the product vision.| |
|We have clearly defined, understand, and agree on the Acceptance Criteria.| |
|We have no blocking open questions.| |
|There are no dependencies blocking completion.| |
|We agree on the Manual and Automation testing approach.| |
|We agree on the customer documentation impacts.| |
|We have relatively sized the Story.| |
|We can complete the Story according to our Workday Definition of Done in a sprint.| |
|We have documented our discussions and the key decision points.| |
|We have discussed toggles| |
